content: The linguistic pedagogy definition of reflexion as a metacognitive learning practice for high school students can be framed as a blueprint to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where dynamic self-correction of LLM outputs mirrors the iterative writing reflection process students learn. We can hypothesize that structuring LLM self-reflection around the same guided, genre-specific reflection prompts used to teach high school expository writing will reduce inconsistent LLM reasoning more effectively than generic self-reflection frameworks.
